rationale: The citing paper references Ansari and Mela (2003) as prior evidence that higher link placement can increase click probability in an email setting. It is used in literature review/background positioning (including grouped citations about attention decay and ordering effects), not as a model, method, or core framework that the paper adopts. The citing study does not build directly on the cited paper's customization or optimization approach, so the role is a contextual mention rather than a substantive dependency.
